description:
## Background

`fz-aiz/any-key-floor` is an unmerged branch (last commit 2026-05-17,
forked 6h before from main at `fz-ul4.43`). It contains 8 source
commits exploring annotation-driven dispatch + a typer soundness
floor. Main has moved a lot since fork (Reduction arc landed: fz-rh5,
fz-9pr, fz-f88; plus fz-yan, fz-s9y, fz-0z4 partial). Some of the
branch's ideas have been overtaken; some are unrealized on main and
still valuable; two are pure bug fixes that should land independently.

This epic is the salvage plan. Each item below is independently
shippable and verified against current main as of commit ca856767.

## Verified state of main (don't re-discover)

- `Parser` struct (parser.rs:192) is minimal — `toks` + `pos` only; no
  context flags. Cond-position parsers exist at expected sites:
  `parse_if`:1536, `parse_case`:1576, `parse_cond`:1613,
  `parse_with`:1717. `when`-guard parsing at parse_fn_clause:737,
  parse_case:1586, parse_with:1769. Call-postfix `do … end` sugar at
  parse_bp:1249.
- `pattern_matrix::find_unreachable_rows` (pattern_matrix.rs:634)
  ignores guards (compiles whole matrix, walks decision tree). `Row`
  has a `guard` field that survives intact.
- `ir_lower::lower_matrix_list_cons` (ir_lower.rs:1426) takes an
  `env_before` snapshot at line 1485 and uses it to filter
  `pat_bind_extras`, but **never restores `ctx.env` / `ctx.env_order`
  across loop iterations**. So clause N's bindings leak into clause
  N+1's snapshot, get filtered out, and lowering emits an unbound
  reference. Confirmed live bug:

  ```fz
  fn f([h | rest]) when h > 0, do: rest
  fn f([h | rest]), do: [h | rest]
  ```

  errors with `error[lower/unbound]: unbound: h`.

- `ModuleTypes.any_key_specs: HashMap<FnId, Vec<Descr>>` exists at
  ir_typer.rs:155 — O(1) index, NOT total over reachable FnIds (only
  populated when the typer's reachability walk genuinely needed an
  any-key, e.g. closure-reachable or entry-seeded). No
  `naturally_opaque` field.
- `callsite_outcome_updates` (ir_typer.rs:167) populates for
  `EmitSlot::Direct | ClosureLit | CallClosureKnown` at line 719.
  **`EmitSlot::Cont | MakeClosure(_)` explicitly skipped at line 729.**
- `apply_callsite_outcomes` (ir_typer.rs:749) merges typer's updates
  into `module.callsite_outcomes` (driver called at
  ir_codegen.rs:1908).
- Codegen still calls `spec_registry.resolve` at four dispatch
  positions: ir_codegen.rs:1573 (closure literal), 2532 (CallClosure
  fn_constants), 2611 (Cont continuation), 2922 (TailCall). Panic
  sites: `.29.12.1` at ir_codegen.rs:3986 (Cont), `.29.11` at 4033
  (Direct/TailCall), `.29.12.2` at 2073 and 6344 (closure target).
- ir_reducer.rs and reducer.rs are part of the finished Reduction
  arc. Reducer publishes `Consumed`/`Stalled` outcomes; typer
  promotes `Stalled → Emitted` for the three slot types it handles.
  This work integrates downstream — no reducer changes needed.

## What lands (sub-tickets, all to be filed under this epic)

### Tier A — standalone bug fixes (no architectural coupling)

**Sub-1: parser cond-context — suppress trailing-do in cond position**

Port of fz-aiz.1. Add `suppress_trailing_do: bool` to `Parser`, gate
the call-postfix sugar at parser.rs:1249–1261, enable via a
`with_no_trailing_do` helper around: `parse_if` cond,
`parse_case` scrutinee, `parse_cond` clause heads, `parse_with`
source, and `when`-guard expressions at the three parse sites
(parse_fn_clause, parse_case, parse_with).

Repro that errors today:
```fz
if pred(h) do
  [h | filter(pred, t)]
else
  filter(pred, t)
end
```
Parses `pred(h) do … end` as `pred(h, do_block)`, leaving `else`
unexpected.

Acceptance: above repro parses and runs. Statement-position trailing-
do (macro DSL forms) still works. 166-fixture matrix green; no
goldens shift.

**Sub-2: pattern_matrix guards + ir_lower env restore (two-bug couple)**

Port of fz-aiz.2. Two coordinated fixes — the pattern-check false
positive (treating guarded rows as covering) masks the lower-side
bug (env leak across cons-row iterations), so fixing only one breaks
fixtures.

  (a) `pattern_matrix::find_unreachable_rows` (pattern_matrix.rs:634):
  fast path preserved when no row has a guard. With guards: walk
  rows in order, test each against the prefix of *unguarded* rows
  only.

  (b) `ir_lower::lower_matrix_list_cons` (ir_lower.rs:1426): snapshot
  `ctx.env` / `ctx.env_order` before the cons-rows loop. Restore
  before each iteration. Final restore after the loop.

Acceptance: the guard repro above runs (prints `[-1, 2, 3]`).
pattern_matrix unit tests cover the three guard-aware cases
(guarded prefix doesn't dominate; unguarded wildcard still
dominates; guarded row unreachable when its pattern is covered by
an unguarded predecessor). Matrix green.

### Tier B — typer soundness floor

**Sub-3: `ensure_any_keys` + `naturally_opaque` split**

Port of fz-aiz.3e. Two coupled changes:

  (a) New typer-internal fn `ensure_any_keys(specs, m)`: after the
  reachability fixpoint converges, walk every FnId with any spec
  and `specs.insert((fid, vec![any; arity]), type_fn(f, m, Some(&any_key)))`
  if absent. Makes `SpecRegistry::resolve` total over reachable
  FnIds via subsumption (`q ⊆ any` for all `q`). Directly retires
  the `.29.11` panic family.

  (b) Add `ModuleTypes.naturally_opaque: HashSet<FnId>` — snapshotted
  from `specs.keys()` BEFORE `ensure_any_keys` augments. Closure
  body picker sites (ir_codegen.rs:6307 MakeClosure +
  closure_shapes builder ~2025) consult `naturally_opaque` for
  routing: opaque-reachable fn → respect resolve (any-key is the
  right answer); direct-only fn → substitute first narrow when
  resolve returns the any-key.

Audit every current reader of `any_key_specs`: under the new model
it's total over reachable FnIds, so callers that meant "does this
fn participate in opaque dispatch" should switch to
`naturally_opaque`. Known readers: ir_typer.rs:184 (`any_key_spec`),
ir_typer.rs:194 (`any_spec_for`), ir_inline.rs:727 (any-key removal
during inlining).

Acceptance: `.29.11` panic on any-key-missing path no longer
reachable. Closure routing parity confirmed against
apply2/higher_order/curried_add/closure_typed_captures/actor_ring/
three_process_chain fixtures. Goldens reblessed (spec table grows
with one any-key per reachable FnId — expected ~5244 new lines in
expected.specs offset by Sub-5's reduction).

### Tier C — codegen reads typer's dispatch decisions

**Sub-4: write `Emitted` outcomes for `EmitSlot::Cont`**

The .29.12.1 root cause. Today, ir_typer.rs:729 explicitly skips
`EmitSlot::Cont`, so codegen falls back to `spec_registry.resolve`
which panics when the typer's flow-narrowed key for the cont
doesn't match codegen's flow-narrowed lookup key.

Change: add `EmitSlot::Cont` to the publishing arm at
ir_typer.rs:719 with the same shape as the others
(`CallsiteOutcome::Emitted { target, came_from: None }`). The walk
already emits cont specs (ir_typer.rs:1517); only the
outcome-publishing step is missing.

Also confirm `MakeClosure(_)` — the closure body picker uses
`spec_registry.resolve` today; if Sub-3 + Sub-6 below handle it
via `naturally_opaque` + outcome read, MakeClosure outcomes may
not need publishing. Decide as part of Sub-6's design.

Acceptance: the original fz-t45 repro (`each(fn(x) -> print(x+k),
[1,2,3])` with `k=10`) compiles past the .29.12.1 panic. Output may
still be wrong if Sub-6 hasn't landed (wire-ABI mismatch in
indirect TailCallClosure path) — that's a separate consumer.

**Sub-5: codegen reads `callsite_outcomes` at the four dispatch sites**

Port of the *idea* of fz-aiz.3c, adapted to main's side-table form
(not the in-band annotation form the branch used).

Sites to migrate, all reading `module.callsite_outcomes[cid].target`
where today they call `spec_registry.resolve`:

  - ir_codegen.rs:1573 (closure literal in lower_prim::MakeClosure)
  - ir_codegen.rs:2532 (CallClosure fn_constants path)
  - ir_codegen.rs:2611 (Cont continuation — depends on Sub-4)
  - ir_codegen.rs:2922 (TailCall)
  - ir_codegen.rs:3986 (Cont resolve in emit_terminator; same panic
    site, may share helper with 2611)

Apply the fz-aiz.3b refactor technique: add the new path, populate
it, keep `resolve` live with `debug_assert_eq!(via_outcome,
via_resolve)`, run the matrix, then swap reads and drop the
asserts in a follow-up commit. Reduces the risk of a silent
dispatch divergence.

After landing: `spec_registry.resolve` calls in codegen reduce to
analysis-only sites (`tagged_return_specs`, closure_shapes
builder); fz-t45's lit_resolved fast path can be reconsidered;
.29.x panics in codegen become `.expect(\"annotation invariant\")`
against the outcome being present.

Acceptance: matrix green with debug-asserts active in one commit;
matrix green with asserts removed in next. No goldens shift.

### Tier D — opt-in diagnostic

**Sub-6: `--Wspec-quality` (or `FZ_WSPEC_QUALITY=1`) diagnostic**

Port of fz-aiz.6. Opt-in walker: for each reachable spec body, at
each direct `Term::Call` / `Term::TailCall`, replicate the
spec_registry resolve. Warn when the resolved spec's key equals
`[any; arity]` AND ≥1 narrower spec exists for the same FnId.
Output: the inferred per-arg Descrs here + the list of narrower
specs registered.

Sits alongside `collect_diagnostics` at ir_typer.rs:2520 as a
sibling walker. Gated behind env var to avoid disturbing standard
build output. CI can enable on curated fixtures.

Useful on its own; far more useful AFTER Sub-3 (otherwise any-keys
don't exist for the direct-only fns that would generate the most
interesting signal).

Acceptance: enabling the flag on the fib_tailrec fixture produces
the expected diagnostic shape (matches fz-aiz.6's example output).
No effect when disabled. Unit test for diagnostic shape.

## Dependency DAG

```
Sub-1  (parser)           — independent
Sub-2  (guards + env)     — independent
Sub-3  (any-key floor)    — independent
Sub-4  (Cont outcomes)    — independent (immediate .29.12.1 relief)
Sub-5  (codegen reads)    — depends on Sub-4 (Cont outcomes must exist)
Sub-6  (Wspec-quality)    — best after Sub-3 (any-keys give signal)
```

Suggested landing order: Sub-1 + Sub-2 in parallel (pure bug fixes,
no risk). Then Sub-4 (one-line typer change unblocking the Cont
panic, no codegen change). Then Sub-3 (adds the floor + routing
field). Then Sub-5 (consumer cutover, with parity asserts). Then
Sub-6 (diagnostic).

## What's explicitly OUT of scope (deferred to fz-7ot or elsewhere)

- The branch's `fz-aiz.7` (drop blanket any-key seed in reachable_specs
  BFS): meaningful only after Sub-5 lands; file as a follow-up once
  the consumer cutover is verified.
- The branch's `fz-aiz.3a` (in-band Option<SpecId> annotations on IR
  Term/Prim variants): superseded by main's side-table form. We use
  `module.callsite_outcomes` instead.
- The branch's `fz-aiz.8` (fixture seeding): per user direction.
- fz-t45's wire-ABI mismatch in the TailCallClosure indirect fallback:
  separate ticket. Sub-4 unblocks observing it; the fix is either the
  ticket's option (B) per-arg coercion or absorption by fz-7ot.
- fz-7ot's canonical-spec-per-fn collapse: a broader architectural
  direction that subsumes parts of Tier C. Track separately.

## Acceptance for the epic

- All six sub-tickets closed.
- `.29.12.1` and `.29.11` panics retired from reachable code paths.
- `ensure_any_keys` lives in the typer; `naturally_opaque` lives on
  `ModuleTypes`; consumers of `any_key_specs` audited.
- Codegen dispatch-critical sites read `module.callsite_outcomes`
  not `spec_registry.resolve`.
- `--Wspec-quality` diagnostic available; gated; documented.
- fz-aiz.1 + fz-aiz.2 repros pass.
- 618 unit + 166 fixture-matrix tests green at each landing.

## See also

- `fz-aiz/any-key-floor` branch (origin of the ideas).
- fz-t45 (the wire-ABI mismatch ticket; Sub-4 unblocks its repro).
- fz-7ot (the broader canonical-spec direction this informs but
  doesn't subsume).

comments.text:
## Rework: .5 is the load-bearing change; .3 dissolves

After in-worktree investigation, the epic structure is wrong. The
any-key floor (.3) treats a symptom: the typer publishes one answer
about which spec applies at each callsite, codegen recomputes the
same answer via subsumption resolve, the two can drift, and the
floor exists so codegen always finds *something* when they do.

The real architectural fix is to stop having codegen ask the
question. The typer already knows which spec applies at each
callsite — it publishes `Emitted { target }` to `module.callsite_outcomes`.
Codegen reading outcomes makes the floor unnecessary: codegen
never queries a key, so it can't fail to find one.

If the outcome IS missing at a callsite codegen needs, that's a
typer/pipeline bug — surfaces as a `.expect("typer must publish
outcome for {cid:?}; this is a typer invariant violation")` panic
at the read site. Legible, debuggable, citable. The any-key floor
was scaffolding to avoid this kind of panic by making resolve
total. With codegen reading published decisions, the panic is the
right answer — it points at the producer, not the consumer.

The closure-body-picker and cont-ABI coupling that bit me on .3
also dissolves: codegen no longer picks `body_func_id` via
subsumption resolve. It reads `Emitted.target` from the
MakeClosure / Cont outcome the typer wrote, and uses that
target's narrow ABI. No naturally_opaque routing field needed;
the typer's outcome IS the routing decision.

### Revised plan

- **.1** ✓ landed (parser cond-context).
- **.2** ✓ landed (pattern_matrix guards + lower env restore).
- **.4** typer publishes outcomes for `EmitSlot::Cont` (and
  reconsider `MakeClosure` — needed by .5's closure read). Pure
  data plumbing; no behavior change in isolation.
- **.5** **(load-bearing)** codegen reads `module.callsite_outcomes`
  at every dispatch site (cont resolve, MakeClosure body picker,
  Call/TailCall callee, closure-lit target). Replace
  `spec_registry.resolve(...)` with `outcome.target → spec_registry.register-or-lookup
  (FnId, key)`. The `.29.11` / `.29.12.1` / `.29.12.2` panic family
  retires; new panics become `expect`s against the published
  outcome being present.
- **.3** REFRAMED to "debug-invariant ticket" — or dissolved
  entirely, since .5's `expect()` *is* the invariant. The
  `naturally_opaque` field disappears: there's nothing to route,
  codegen reads the typer's chosen target directly. The any-key
  body for opaque-dispatch fns (spawn/Receive/unresolved
  CallClosure) still gets created naturally by the typer's
  reachability pass — but that's just "the typer's existing
  behavior for opaque consumers," not a new floor.
- **.6** **(diagnostic)** `--Wspec-quality` is still useful, but
  its meaning shifts: it now warns when the *typer's published
  outcome* selects the any-key while narrower specs exist for the
  same callee. Same signal, different read site. Independent of
  the rest.

### Dependency DAG (revised)

```
.1 (parser)              — landed
.2 (pattern + lower)     — landed
.4 (publish Cont/MakeClosure outcomes) — pre-req for .5
.5 (codegen reads outcomes) — LOAD-BEARING — closes the loop
.3 — closed (dissolved into .5)
.6 (Wspec-quality)       — independent, retargets to read outcomes
```

### Why .3 dissolves rather than survives

If .5 is correctly done, the only way codegen reaches a "no
outcome" path is via a typer bug. A debug-build assertion would
catch the same condition `.5`'s `expect()` catches. .3 would be a
no-op in well-formed pipelines and duplicate effort with `.5`.

Where .3 *would* have been load-bearing — the any-key as a
soundness *fallback* — is now just a panic. We don't want a
fallback; we want the typer to be authoritative. If the typer
isn't, that's the bug to fix, not the gap to paper over.

### What I learned trying to land .3

- `ensure_any_keys` iterating `specs.keys()` misses FnIds that
  are reachable as IR (MakeClosure target etc.) but absent from
  `specs` — those are the cases that panic downstream.
- Adding any-keys for cont fns breaks slot-0 ABI (cont ABI is
  per-spec). Naturally_opaque was meant to thread the routing
  signal through codegen but compounds the coupling.
- The closure body picker has to substitute the narrow spec when
  resolve returns the any-key for a non-opaque fn — another
  thread of the same problem.
- All three concerns vanish when codegen reads the typer's
  outcome directly: the typer points at the narrow target by
  construction (it knows the captures, the call shape, the
  caller's narrowing). No subsumption, no routing, no floor.

The salvage value of the worktree work is the .1 and .2 commits
(both confirmed bugs, both pass tests). The .3 attempt is dropped
in favor of the cleaner .5 architecture.

title: fz-aiz salvage: port unmerged ideas from the any-key floor branch
